summary: Iteration 55 — FINAL App Store validation. BACKEND: 16/16 pytest passed including the critical APPLE_PRODUCT_TO_PLAN mapping test (verified via direct import that 'PRO_Plus' -> 'pro' NOT 'pro_plus', legacy 'PRO'/'PROYearly'/'Pro_simple_yearly' also -> 'pro'), /api/iap/verify-apple-receipt accepts product_id='PRO_Plus' under auth and returns success:false (not 500) for bogus receipts, /api/v2/quota returns is_pro/contacts_limit/prospecting_limit_per_week, /api/v2/prospecting/listings?sector=75003&kind=apartment returned 200 in 11s with items:[]+source:'scraping_in_progress' (no 502/Cloudflare), /api/v2/ai/chat (Claude via emergentintegrations) returned reply in ~13s, all V2 CRUD (cases/notes/reminders/contacts) accept payloads with correct Pydantic shapes (cases.type ∈ {'seller','buyer'}, reminders requires 'date', contacts requires 'first_name'). FRONTEND (Playwright mobile 430x932 on preview URL): /app-v2/login renders with email input, 'Recevoir un code par email' button, 'Continuer avec Apple' button, 'Continuer avec Google' button, K logo on light mesh gradient bg (rose/blue/green/yellow) — looks 'wow'. After injecting session_token to localStorage and onboarding via API, /app-v2/settings/subscription shows title 'Passe en Pro', price '24,99 €', 7 green checkmarks (perks), data-testid='sub-purchase-btn' with text "S'abonner — 24,99 €/mois", and data-testid='sub-restore-btn' is COUNT=0 (correctly hidden in web — only shown when isIOSNative() — Apple §3.1.1 compliant). /app-v2 home renders 'Bonjour' greeting, Activity Rings (home-ring-reminders + home-ring-notes), Daily Advice card, Ask KOLO pill with lucide-brain icon (SVG class 'lucide lucide-brain'), bottom nav with central mic FAB. /app-v2/notifications renders WITHOUT 404 (title 'Notifications', 'Tu es à jour', RAPPELS DU JOUR section, push-notification CTA). CODE REVIEW (file reads): /app/backend/server.py:1506-1513 APPLE_PRODUCT_TO_PLAN={'PRO_Plus':'pro', 'PRO':'pro', 'Pro_simple_yearly':'pro', 'PROYearly':'pro'} ✓; /app/frontend/src/services/iapStore.js:22-26 PRODUCT_IDS.pro_monthly=pro_plus_monthly=pro_yearly=pro_plus_yearly='PRO_Plus' ✓; /app/frontend/src/App.js:130 contains `Capacitor.isNativePlatform() ? <Navigate to="/app-v2" replace /> : <LandingPageNew />` ✓; /app/frontend/src/App.js:168 route /app-v2/settings/subscription -> V2SubscriptionPage ✓; V2SubscriptionPage.js line 25 `const native = isIOSNative();`, line 155 sub-purchase-btn, line 161 `{native && (...sub-restore-btn...)}` ✓.

issues: AI CTA text reads 'Ask KOLO' in en-US browser locale (Playwright default navigator.language=en-US) — v2t('askKolo') returns the EN string. In French browsers it reads 'Demande à KOLO' per v2i18n.js. Same as iter54 — not a bug, structural pass via lucide-brain icon + data-testid.

critical_code_review_comments: APPLE_PRODUCT_TO_PLAN mapping is correct (PRO_Plus -> 'pro'). Apple App Store Connect product 'PRO_Plus' (display 'KOLO PRO' 24,99€/mois) will correctly upgrade users to plan='pro' after verifyReceipt.

/app/frontend/src/services/iapStore.js consolidates all 4 PRODUCT_IDS keys (pro_monthly, pro_plus_monthly, pro_yearly, pro_plus_yearly) to single 'PRO_Plus' product. APPLE_PRODUCT_REVERSE_MAP at line 41 maps 'PRO_Plus' -> 'pro_plus' which is a string-only label used in iapStore.js; backend mapping is the source of truth for actual plan assignment. No conflict because purchasePlan('pro') -> PRODUCT_IDS.pro_monthly='PRO_Plus' -> backend maps PRO_Plus->'pro'. The roundtrip is correct.

/app/frontend/src/App.js:130 Capacitor redirect verified — when running in iOS native, '/' navigates to /app-v2 (skipping landing marketing) as required. Cannot test in browser (Capacitor.isNativePlatform() is always false in Playwright web context).
V2SubscriptionPage.js correctly gates Restaurer mes achats behind `{native && ...}` (line 161) ensuring §3.1.1 compliance: no non-StoreKit payment UI exposed on iOS native unless restore.
v2_router.py prospecting still uses range(6)*2s polling — verified <15s on preview. Cloudflare-safe.
Backend server.py is 8445 lines — very large monolith. Recommend splitting into modules in a future iteration (iap.py, prospecting.py, billing.py).

20 transient 401s observed in browser console during home load — they happen when components fetch before token sync completes; backend rejects then UI retries. Could be reduced by guarding fetches on isAuthenticated() but not user-visible. Already noted in iter54.
test_credentials: Backend uses fresh iter55.<uuid>@kolo-test.io per test run (auto-onboards). Frontend test injects session_token to localStorage after backend auth + onboarding (token key: v2_session_token + session_token + token to cover all consumers).
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed. Backend pytest fixture creates a fresh email per module + onboarding payload; orphaned test users remain in db.users_v2 (prefixed iter55.).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_55.py -v --tb=short. Critical: APPLE mapping test imports server.py directly (sys.path.insert /app/backend), so it runs in seconds. CRUD endpoints have strict Pydantic schemas: cases.type ∈ {'seller','buyer'} (NOT 'vente'/'achat'), reminders requires 'date' not 'due_at', contacts requires 'first_name' (not 'name'). Stripe checkout endpoint is /api/payments/create-checkout (NOT /api/v2/billing/create-checkout-session — that path 404s). For Playwright UI testing of auth-gated V2 pages, the reliable pattern is: (1) call /api/v2/auth/send-email-code + read dev_code (2) call /api/v2/auth/verify-email-code to get session_token (3) call /api/v2/onboarding (4) inject token to localStorage under keys 'v2_session_token', 'session_token', 'token' (5) goto /app-v2/whatever. The UI verify-code flow has only 1 code input field which may auto-submit on completion but in Playwright we sometimes don't reach the home — bypass via direct API auth + localStorage. iOS Capacitor redirect is unverifiable in browser; confirmed by reading App.js line 130. IAP actual purchase only testable in Apple TestFlight sandbox.
rca of the issue: No blockers. The only ambiguity in the review_request was the listed endpoint /api/v2/billing/create-checkout-session which 404s — actual web Stripe path is /api/payments/create-checkout. App Store §3.1.1 compliance verified at code level: on iOS native, the redirect (App.js:130) sends user directly into /app-v2 bypassing landing marketing, and V2SubscriptionPage exposes ONLY the IAP purchase button (iapStore.purchasePlan -> StoreKit) — the web Stripe path is unreachable from native UI. Restore button (sub-restore-btn) is correctly gated to isIOSNative() only. Ready for Apple submit.
